title: 'Do a good present' — May 2021 title-only aphorism
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from May 2, 2021, 1:32am — Yahir is 18, Grants Pass senior, three weeks after the Ford OSU prep stack and three weeks before his Al Forthan speech deadline. Title is the entire artifact: "Do a good present, and you won't have to worry about the future." Body is empty. 29-second create→modify gap.
  • Pure manifesto style note aphorism in the terminal-form. The title is the thesis; no body follows. The 1:32am timestamp suggests a late-night-before-bed thought capture, not a deliberate essay.
  • Sits in the 2021 single-line-thesis season alongside "Love is sacrificing everything" and "Setting your heart ablaze" — Yahir running personal-philosophy compression experiments inside the same fall/spring as the Ford / Al Forthan / OSU application crunch.
  • The aphorism is the present-action variant of the disconnected from outcome frame — both relocate worry from outcomes to inputs. Companion to max effort commit (do the work, the rest follows).
  • Anticipates the much later "hard things I have done" retrospective frame — the inverse-direction version of the same logic (look at what you did, not what you feared).

Source

"Do a good present, and you won't have to worry about the future"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Cleanest pre-OSU articulation of Yahir's present-action-over-future-anxiety disposition — the operating principle that drives the fear of not doing / max effort commit axis.
  • The aphorism format itself proves the manifesto style note habit was already mature at 18 — a year before the OSU-era density bloom.

Open questions

  • Yahir's own composition or a quote from a May-2021 source (book / podcast / speech)? No surrounding context.
